subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Tornado Formation
description: A micro-lesson that makes Tornado Formation usable.

hook.text: A tornado can spin at 300 mph—stronger than any Category 5 hurricane. It forms in minutes.

buildup.text: Tornadoes form when warm, moist air meets cold, dry air, creating a rotating updraft called a mesocyclone.

discovery.text: Wind shear—wind changing speed and direction with altitude—tilts the rotation vertical.

twist.text: Only 20% of supercell thunderstorms produce tornadoes. Predicting which ones is still imprecise.

climax.text: Enhanced Fujita scale rates damage from EF0 (light) to EF5 (incredible destruction).

punchline.text: Warm meets cold, wind shears, funnel drops. Which storm? Unpredictable.

quiz.question: What atmospheric condition is essential for tornado formation?
options: Wind shear creating rotation when warm and cold air masses meet
